modules.summary: Handles user authentication by accepting credentials, validating them, displaying relevant error banners for failed attempts, and redirecting authenticated users to the Product Inventory page.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the application's entry point located at the sign-in page; after successful authentication it transitions users into the Product Inventory area where the persistent header and hamburger menu are available. It sits before all shopping flows (Product Detail, Cart, and Checkout steps) and is referenced by the initial navigation path from sign-in → Product Inventory.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and at the sign-in page with Username and Password fields available.

modules.summary: Displays a list of products with name, description, price, sorting controls, and buttons to add or remove items from the shopping cart.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the main landing page after successful sign-in (Sign-in → Product Inventory) and acts as the central browsing hub with a persistent header and hamburger menu; from here users can open Product Detail, add items which update the cart badge, navigate to the Shopping Cart, and continue the Checkout flow (Information → Overview → Confirmation).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in (post-sign-in) with an active session.

modules.summary: Shows detailed information for a selected product (image, name, description, price) and an add/remove control that reflects the current cart state.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from the Product Inventory by clicking a product name or image, this page is part of the product-browsing flow and sits between Inventory and the Shopping Cart/Checkout path; the persistent header (hamburger menu and cart icon) remains available for navigation back to All Items, the cart, or app-level actions like Logout or Reset App State.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with a product selected from Product Inventory and an active app session (cart may contain items).

modules.summary: Lists items added to the cart with quantity, description, and per-item remove controls, and provides actions to continue shopping or begin the checkout flow.
modules.where_it_fits: Reached from the Product Inventory (via the header shopping cart icon or after adding items), the Shopping Cart sits between browsing and the multi-step Checkout flow. It functions as the review/modify step before entering Checkout - Information → Checkout - Overview → Checkout - Confirmation.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with one or more items already added to the cart.

modules.summary: Collects the customer's first name, last name, and ZIP/postal code and validates required fields before advancing to the checkout overview.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the first step of the three-step checkout flow (Information → Overview → Confirmation) reached from the Shopping Cart after users proceed with checkout; it precedes the Checkout - Overview module and follows the Shopping Cart in the purchase lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with at least one item in the shopping cart and checkout initiated from the Shopping Cart.

modules.summary: Displays an order summary with cart items, payment and shipping details, and totals, allowing the user to finish or cancel the checkout process.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the middle step of the three-step checkout flow reached from the Product Inventory -> Shopping Cart -> Checkout (Information → Overview → Confirmation). It follows the Checkout - Information step and precedes Checkout - Confirmation, completing the order when Finish is used or exiting checkout when Cancel is used.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with items in the shopping cart who has completed the Checkout - Information step (checkout flow initiated)

modules.summary: Displays an order success message and a Back Home action that returns to the Product Inventory and clears the cart.
modules.where_it_fits: This is the final step of the checkout flow reached after Product Inventory → Shopping Cart → Checkout - Information → Checkout - Overview → Checkout - Confirmation, serving as the completion page before returning the user to the Product Inventory. It concludes the purchase lifecycle and resets the cart state for further browsing.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has completed the Checkout - Overview step with an order submitted.

modules.summary: Ends the current authenticated session and returns the user to the login page, preventing access to protected pages until re-authentication.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the hamburger menu in the persistent header after sign-in (hamburger menu options: All Items, About, Logout, Reset App State), Logout is part of session management and is used at any point after landing on the Product Inventory or other protected pages to terminate the session and send the user back to the sign-in screen.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: An active authenticated user session (logged in) on a protected page such as Product Inventory, Product Detail, Shopping Cart, or any Checkout step.

modules.summary: Clears the shopping cart and resets in-app UI state (for example, cart badge and add/remove button states) without logging the user out.
modules.where_it_fits: Available from the hamburger menu in the persistent header on post-login pages (e.g., Product Inventory), this option resets the application's runtime state across inventory and cart flows. It is part of the post-login navigation alongside All Items, About, and Logout and is used before or between checkout steps to restore default UI/state.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with an active session and the application has mutable state (e.g., items added to the cart or changed add/remove button states).
